verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} defines the rules and acceptance criteria that {target} pattern or disposition must follow (e.g., specification section governs submittal review cycle, NFPA governs inspection disposition criteria)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} requirement causes {target} status transition when a condition is met or violated (e.g., specification non-compliance triggers revise-and-resubmit, OSHA hazard finding triggers escalation)
verbGlossary.DISPOSITIONS TO: {source}'s acceptance criteria determine the resulting disposition {target} of a reviewed item (e.g., specification section compliance results in approved-as-noted disposition)
verbGlossary.SCOPES: {source}'s classification divisions define what is included in an inspection or review {target} (e.g., MasterFormat divisions scope which trades are inspected in each pass)
verbGlossary.STRUCTURES: {source} provides the organizational framework that defines {target}s or financial review stages (e.g., MasterFormat structures inspection workflow steps by trade, AACE structures multi-stage cost review)
verbGlossary.VALIDATES DURING: {source}'s code assignments are verified for correctness and completeness during a financial {target} stage (e.g., cost code accuracy validated during budget stage before approval)
verbGlossary.ROUTES THROUGH: {source}'s breakdown structure determines {target} routing path for financial documents (e.g., WBS hierarchy routes budget items through sequential approval by level)
verbGlossary.LOCKED UPON: {source}'s assigned classification codes become immutable once {target} reaches an approved status (e.g., WBS code assignment locked upon budget approval to prevent reclassification)
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHED DURING: {source}'s code structure is set up and mapped to project line items during a financial {target} stage (e.g., budget codes established during budget stage as part of project setup)
verbGlossary.CITES: {source}'s hazard or violation category is formally referenced when a field {target} of deficient is recorded (e.g., OSHA fall protection hazard cited on a deficient inspection finding)
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BEFORE: {source}'s recordkeeping obligations must be satisfied before {target} can reach its terminal status (e.g., OSHA 300 log entries required before incident record can be closed)
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES: {source}mandates that an inspection be placed in ready-for-review {target} at specific construction milestones (e.g., IBC requires structural inspection readied before concrete pour)
verbGlossary.CERTIFIES TO: {source}'s pass criteria are the basis for a conforming field disposition (e.g., IBC requirements met certifies to conforming {target}, energy code commissioning test certifies to conforming)
verbGlossary.PRESCRIBES: {source}defines the required {target} chain structure for document and change management workflows (e.g., AIA contract documents prescribe the architect-owner-contractor approval chain)
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source} contractually requires a specific {target} mechanism such as electronic signature (e.g., AIA contract provisions mandate e-signature for executed agreements)
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: Delivery method classification determines which {target} pattern applies to a project's review processes (e.g., design-build delivery method determines parallel review instead of sequential)
verbGlossary.SUBMITS TO: {source}requires documentation to enter a formal review {target} for third-party evaluation (e.g., LEED credit documentation submitted to under-review for GBCI assessment)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} measure's classification is used to break down {target} performance metric for comparative analysis (e.g., safety classification completeness segments overdue rate by hazard type, MasterFormat coverage segments first-pass approval rate by division)
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} adoption metric trends in relation to {target} performance measure (e.g., higher WBS adoption rate correlates with faster average approval cycle time, certification adoption correlates with workflow completion rate)
